board_csa_architect.notes: Infrastructure-only change. No schema impact. Healthchecks follow established DEV tier pattern. Aligns with PROD topology in INFRA-ENV-MANIFEST. No scalability concern — 2 containers only.

board_ciso_cybersecurity.notes: Webhook URL from env var only — no secrets in compose files. Alert payloads contain no PII (service name, timestamp, status only). No RLS or auth surface changes. Discord webhook is outbound-only. APPROVE.

board_caio_fullstack.notes: No AI/prompt/VRAM impact. Pure infrastructure config. Health scripts are ops-level bash — no model loading or inference calls. Clean separation from AI layer.

board_vp_ops_devops.notes: Critical for production reliability. on-failure:5 prevents crash-loop storms. Discord webhook provides immediate visibility. Health check scripts enable runbook automation. Passes 3AM test — alerts wake you up before users notice.

board_red_team.notes: Failure scenarios: (1) Discord webhook rate-limit causes alert loss — mitigate with local log fallback. (2) Health script false positive during managed service maintenance — add retry+backoff. (3) restart on-failure:5 exhausted with no alert — ensure alert fires BEFORE Docker gives up. STRIDE: Spoofing=N/A (outbound only), Tampering=LOW (env var injection), Repudiation=LOW (alerts are logged), InfoDisclosure=LOW (no PII in payloads), DoS=MED (alert flooding), EoP=N/A. Anti-AC additions: MUST NEVER flood webhook (rate-limit to max 1 alert per service per 5min), MUST NEVER suppress alerts silently if webhook fails (fall back to stderr).
